label: Jaeger
description: Query and explore distributed traces from a Jaeger backend for observability and performance analysis.

form.title: Connect Jaeger
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Jaeger Query endpoint to search and visualize distributed traces.

methods.label: Jaeger Query URL
methods.description: Paste the base URL of your Jaeger Query service. Works for open instances; add optional basic-auth credentials for password-protected deployments.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Jaeger Query URL

Jaeger exposes a query API (and UI) from a single endpoint. Anton talks directly to that API.

### Steps

1. Find the URL where your Jaeger Query service is running — typically the same host you open in a browser to see the Jaeger UI (e.g. `http://jaeger.internal:16686`).
2. If your instance is open (no auth), paste that URL into **Jaeger Query URL** and leave the credential fields empty.
3. If your instance is protected by HTTP basic authentication:
   - Obtain the username and password from whoever deployed the instance (Kubernetes ingress, nginx, etc.).
   - Fill in **Username** and **Password** below.
4. Click **Submit**. Anton will call `<query_url>/api/services` to verify connectivity.

### Notes

- The default Jaeger Query HTTP port is **16686**. gRPC is on 16685 — Anton uses the HTTP API, so use the HTTP port.
- If your deployment sits behind a TLS-terminating proxy, use `https://` in the URL.
- If Jaeger is behind an OAuth/OIDC proxy (e.g. oauth2-proxy), use the **Bearer Token** method instead.
- Anton does not store or forward your credentials beyond the local vault.

fields.name: query_url
fields.label: Jaeger Query URL
fields.description: Base URL of the Jaeger Query service — no trailing slash. Anton appends /api/... paths.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Basic Auth Username
fields.description: Leave blank if your Jaeger instance does not require authentication.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Basic Auth Password
fields.description: HTTP basic-auth password. Only needed for instances protected by an authenticating proxy.

methods.label: Bearer Token (OAuth / OIDC Proxy)
methods.description: For Jaeger instances behind an OAuth2 or OIDC reverse proxy that expects an Authorization header. Paste your query URL and a long-lived bearer token.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Bearer Token

Some Kubernetes-hosted Jaeger deployments sit behind an oauth2-proxy, Istio RBAC policy, or similar middleware that validates a bearer token on every request.

### Steps

1. Obtain a token with read access to the Jaeger Query API:
   - **OIDC / Keycloak**: generate a service-account token from your identity provider.
   - **Kubernetes**: create a ServiceAccount, bind it to the appropriate role, and retrieve its token via `kubectl create token <sa>`.
   - **oauth2-proxy**: follow your org's IdP flow to get a long-lived access token.
2. Copy the token string (without the `Bearer ` prefix — Anton adds that automatically).
3. Paste your Jaeger Query base URL into **Jaeger Query URL**.
4. Paste the token into **Bearer Token**.
5. Click **Submit**. Anton sends `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every request.

### Notes

- Short-lived tokens (e.g. Kubernetes default 1-hour tokens) will stop working after expiry. Re-paste a fresh token when that happens.
- If your proxy uses a different header name (e.g. `X-Auth-Token`), this method may not work — contact your platform team for an alternative credential type.

fields.name: query_url
fields.label: Jaeger Query URL
fields.description: Base URL of the Jaeger Query service — no trailing slash.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer Token
fields.description: Access token passed in the Authorization: Bearer header. Do not include the 'Bearer ' prefix.
